questions.question: A Bayesian network model is trained to predict the probability of a person getting sick given their symptoms. The model achieves an accuracy of 85% on a test dataset. What does this mean in terms of the model's performance?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of accuracy in the context of Bayesian networks

questions.question: A Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm is used to approximate the posterior distribution of a Bayesian network. The MCMC algorithm converges to the true posterior distribution within 100 iterations. What does this indicate about the model's performance?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of convergence in MCMC algorithms

questions.question: A Bayesian network model is used to predict the likelihood of a person buying a car given their age, income, and occupation. The model achieves a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.1. What does this mean in terms of the model's performance?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of RMSE and its application in evaluating predictive models
